goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena. You said you're feeling a 64 this morning, which is a bit lower than usual — let's start by honoring that.

Last night you got a **solid 7.9 hours** with really efficient sleep. That's a great foundation. But your sleep architecture was a bit off — deep sleep was high while REM was lower, which can happen when stress is up or you've had late caffeine. And speaking of yesterday, your stress hit **85/100** and your energy was reported at a really low 18. That's a huge disconnect from what your body is showing: your overnight HRV was a strong **132.6**, meaning physically you recovered well. This mismatch — feeling drained while your body is ready — is the core thing to navigate today.

Your nutrition yesterday was substantial (2379 kcal, 118g protein) but the timing compressed your night: a meal and caffeine at 11:08 PM, right before an early bedtime. That likely contributed to the sleep imbalance and added to the stress-energy strain.

Today is about **resetting that timing and giving your nervous system some space.** We're focusing on cutting caffeine early and creating a calm evening buffer to protect your sleep, while having a simple tool ready for when tension pops up.

Your body is recovered — today's work is to help your mind catch up.

assumptions: User is observing Ramadan fasting patterns
Sleep onset timing improvements are feasible
User can adjust meal timing slightly
Nutrition logging confidence is moderate (0.58)
Journal data is missing but not critical for today's constraints
hard_constraints: protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM
avoid high-intensity exercise given sleep debt and low recovery
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
prioritize gentle movement over intense activity due to energy score (18) and stress (85)

generated_actions.title: Stop caffeine by 12:00 PM today
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's last meal was extremely late (11:08 PM) and included 75mg of caffeine from an iced latte. This compressed your fasting window before an early sleep onset (~1:59 AM) and likely contributed to the sleep architecture imbalance noted (low REM 23.44%). Today's action directly addresses the 'late_caffeine' risk flag and the cross-domain signal of 'late_eating_with_caffeine_compromising_sleep_preparation' to protect tonight's sleep quality.

evaluation.success_definition: No caffeine is logged after 2:00 PM today, breaking yesterday's pattern of a late iced latte at 11:08 PM.

assumptions: You consume caffeine in the morning
You can monitor timing
generated_actions.description: When you have your morning coffee or tea, make it your last caffeine source for the day. Do not consume any caffeinated drinks after 12:00 PM local time.

generated_actions.title: Take a 5-minute 'alertness reset' when you feel tension rising
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's subjective state was severely strained with extreme stress (85) and emotions 'Alert' and 'Tense'. Your stress is at a +0.76 regime shift and sharply rising (+7.81 slope_fast). This implementation intention leverages your reported alertness for a sensory reset, directly addressing the 'burnout_risk' and 'volatility' flags by providing a concrete, immediate coping tool.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 5-minute 'alertness reset' when you felt tension today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least one intentional 5-minute sensory break when tension or the 'Tense' emotion arose.

assumptions: You can take brief micro-breaks
You have access to a window or view
generated_actions.description: When you notice the 'Tense' emotion (which you reported yesterday) or feel your shoulders tightening, immediately step away for 5 minutes. Stand up, look out a window, and name 3 things you see in detail, while taking slow breaths.

generated_actions.title: Log your Iftar meal immediately after eating
generated_actions.rationale: YESTERDAY's nutrition logging confidence was moderate (0.58) and has a slightly positive trend (+0.02 slope_fast), but the 'nutrition_gap' and 'low_data_confidence' risk flags persist. This tiny, specific action builds on yesterday's successful logging (5 meals) to reinforce the habit and address the soft constraint 'follow-up on nutrition confidence gap (0.58)'.

evaluation.success_definition: At least one meal is logged today (meal_count_day > 0), maintaining the improved nutrition data streak from yesterday.

assumptions: You will eat an Iftar meal
You have access to a nutrition logging app or method
generated_actions.description: Right after you finish your Ramadan Iftar meal tonight, open your nutrition app and log a brief description (e.g., 'Grilled chicken, rice, vegetables'). Don't worry about exact amounts—just capture the meal.

generated_actions.title: Start a 60-minute screen-free wind-down at 7:00 PM
generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep was long and efficient (7.9h, 92.58% efficiency) with an early onset (~1:59 AM, -1.71 regime shift). To reinforce this positive trend and protect your 'evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM' hard constraint, this action creates a protected buffer to support another early, high-quality sleep, directly aligning with your sleep goal domain and today's 'sleep_consistency_and_recovery' priority.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you initiate a screen-free wind-down starting at 7:00 PM tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports starting a screen-free, low-stimulation wind-down routine at or close to 7:00 PM.

assumptions: You can adjust your evening schedule
You have access to low-light activities
generated_actions.description: When 7:00 PM arrives, put away all screens (phone, computer, TV). Dim the lights and spend the next 60 minutes on gentle, non-stimulating activities like reading a physical book, light tidying, or gentle stretching.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 7:00 PM alarm to stop screens and begin a 75-minute wind-down
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep onset was unusually early (~3:11 AM), representing a -1.56 regime shift. However, sleep architecture was imbalanced (high deep sleep 28.7%, low REM 19.28%). Today's priority is 'sleep_recovery_and_stability' and the hard constraint is to 'protect evening wind-down window starting by 7:00 PM'. This extended wind-down creates a protected buffer to aim for an even earlier, more balanced sleep tonight, directly addressing the 'sleep_debt' risk flag and reinforcing your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:00 PM alarm help you initiate a screen-free wind-down tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 7:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1140) AND user reports using the alarm to start a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You have access to an alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When your alarm rings at 7:00 PM, turn off all screens (phone, computer, TV). Dim the lights and spend the next 75 minutes doing gentle, non-stimulating activities like reading a physical book, light tidying, or stretching.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, write down one positive intention for the day
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Your self-reported score (75) exceeds the physiological proxy (53) by 22 points—a pattern where you rate yourself higher than proxy 71.4% of the time. This cognitive-action (writing an intention) anchors to your morning routine to gently bridge the subjective-objective gap, addressing the 'mismatch_pattern' risk flag and promoting stability through intentionality.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you write a positive intention after your morning coffee today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing the intention-writing exercise after their morning coffee.

assumptions: You drink morning coffee
You have access to a pen/paper or note app
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Right after you finish your morning coffee, take 2 minutes to write one sentence on a piece of paper or digital note about a positive intention for today (e.g., 'I will maintain calm during my meetings'). Keep it visible.

This tiny, specific action directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' and 'low_data_confidence' risk flags, and the soft constraint 'follow-up on nutrition confidence gap (0)'. evaluation.success_definition: At least one meal is logged today (meal_count_day > 0), breaking the streak of zero nutrition data.

assumptions: You have access to a nutrition logging app or method
You will eat at least one meal
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When you eat your first meal (Iftar), immediately open your nutrition logging app and record a brief description (e.g., 'Chicken, rice, vegetables'). Don't worry about exact amounts—just capture the meal.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Do 5 minutes of gentle stretching before getting into bed
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: LAST NIGHT's sleep architecture showed high deep sleep (28.7%) but low REM (19.28%). Gentle pre-bed stretching can promote physical relaxation and may help balance sleep stages, supporting your sleep goal domain and the cross-domain signal 'deep_dominant_sleep_with_low_rem'. It also respects the hard constraint 'avoid high-intensity exercise given sleep debt and low recovery'.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 5 minutes of gentle stretching before getting into bed tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing gentle stretching immediately before bed.

assumptions: You have a safe space to stretch near your bed
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When you are ready to get into bed tonight, spend 5 minutes doing very gentle stretching—focus on your neck, shoulders, and calves. Move slowly and breathe deeply.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Hey Ameena. You're feeling solid this morning with a 75 score and high energy mode — that's a great start.

**Your day in context**
Last night you got 6.82 hours, which is above your recent average and rising. Your sleep was efficient and packed with deep sleep (28.7%), but your REM was unusually low at 19.28%. That imbalance can happen when you're recovering, and it might leave you feeling a bit mentally foggy today. Your body’s recovery signals are strong — HRV jumped to 95.19 and is climbing sharply. That high recovery happened alongside a day where you barely moved: just 5,328 steps and zero MVPA yesterday. It looks like you took a real rest day, and your body used it to rebuild. The early sleep onset (~3:11 AM) after such low activity suggests you might have been genuinely tired or intentionally winding down early. This pattern of you feeling better than your physiological metrics shows up again — your self-report is 22 points higher than the proxy. That’s a persistent gap, but it’s narrowing slightly.
Given all this, today is about protecting that recovery momentum while gently nudging your system toward balance.

**Action bridge**
Your actions are designed to anchor your positive mindset, lock in an earlier wind-down, and start filling in your nutrition picture.

**Closer**
You’ve got strong autonomic recovery backing you up — today’s about using that foundation to build a more balanced day.

evidence_quotes: It was a stressful day and its always stressful not knowing what will happen next
I couldn't really focus on anything very kuxh

meal_descriptions.description: A banana and a beef samosa
A spoon of creamy pasta and a thin slice of beef steak
Chocolate croissant and an iced latte
Grilled chicken breast and fries and a slice of cheese kunafa
2 cheese omelet a pancake a hashbrown and 3 slices of turkey bacon.
